metadata.title: Why a backend test that passes in reality stays permanently "failing"
metadata.topic: Verification gates that pattern-match the wrong artifact
metadata.purpose: Gates accept proof by artifact pattern; a test category whose real proof is a different artifact (shell result vs. screenshot) silently fails, and the agent learns to avoid the whole category.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: backend-verify.cjs internals; the phase-ordering prompt fix
metadata.visual_object: Two verification paths forking — screenshot branch and text-result branch — both converging on one "test passes" decision.
metadata.hook: Zero backend tests passed — not because the agent couldn't write them, but because they couldn't be marked passing without a screenshot.

beats.narration_text: The verification gate required a Playwright screenshot as proof. Backend code produces no browser output, so every backend test stayed "unverified"; the agent rationally stopped writing them. Adding a text-artifact path unlocked 18 backend + 36 frontend tests at once.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: How does a test that succeeds at runtime remain permanently failing until the verification *format* — not the code — changes?
Zero backend tests passed — not because the agent couldn't write them, but because they couldn't be marked passing without a screenshot.
Gates accept proof by artifact pattern; a test category whose real proof is a different artifact (shell result vs. screenshot) silently fails, and the agent learns to avoid the whole category.
beats.visual: Two verification paths forking — screenshot branch and text-result branch — both converging on one "test passes" decision.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Rule: "passes only if a screenshot.txt exists." A DynamoDB test runs, responds correctly, produces no screenshot → marked failing. Add "also passes if result.txt contains VERIFIED_BY: backend-verify.cjs" → agent writes 18 backend tests.
Gates accept proof by artifact pattern; a test category whose real proof is a different artifact (shell result vs. screenshot) silently fails, and the agent learns to avoid the whole category.
